response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering breadth and mastery of multiple technology stacks, the role has critical blocker requirements. Native-level Japanese proficiency is completely absent and is explicitly listed as required. Additionally, strong C programming expertise for UNIX embedded systems is severely lacking—only 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments in 2014-2015, with primary experience in JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and C#. Most critically, there is no background in embedded firmware, security technologies (encryption, authentication systems, attack detection), or networking (Layer-2/3, switch operation, SNMP/NETCONF). Recent roles are web/backend-focused, not embedded systems or security-critical development.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience (15+ years) with demonstrated ability to learn and master diverse technology stacks and domains
Strong foundation in software craftsmanship including TDD, CI/CD, infrastructure automation, and DevOps (Docker, GitLab, deployment systems)
Proven ability to take ownership of complex projects and deliver high-impact solutions across multiple product areas
concerns: Native-level Japanese language proficiency (required) is completely absent—resume lists only English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and German (basic)
Embedded systems and C programming expertise severely lacking—minimal C/C++ work from 2014-2015; primary languages are JavaScript, TypeScript, and DLang
No background in security technologies (encryption, authentication, attack detection), networking fundamentals (Layer-2/3, switch operation, SNMP, NETCONF), or firmware development

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C programming and embedded systems experience
No security or networking domain background
